title: Time use survey 2009/10 – supplementary tables | Stats NZ
content:
## Time use survey 2009/10 – supplementary tables
19 October 2011, 10:45am
Download time use supplementary tables that are extensions of the Time use survey: 2009/10 information release tables. They contain variables for highest qualification, personal income, household income, region, urban/rural, and NZ Deprivation index quintiles that are not covered in the release commentary . These tables contain data from the Time use survey: 2009/10 and Time use survey: 1998/99 .
Please consider the information in the sample errors files and data quality notes when analysing data from these tables.
Time use survey: 2009/10 – supplementary tables 2 and 4 (Excel, 16 sheets, 230 KB)
Time use survey: 2009/10 – supplementary table 9 (Excel, 13 sheets, 187 KB)
Time use survey: 2009/10 – sampling error supplementary tables 2 and 4 (Excel, 16 sheets, 238 KB)
Time use survey: 2009/10 – sampling error supplementary table 9 (Excel, 13 sheets, 198 KB)

### Supplementary tables 2 and 4
Supplementary tables 2 and 4 cover mean time spent per day for the total population. Included are:
- ST 2.1a Time spent on primary activities, all males, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- ST 2.1b Time spent on primary activities, all females, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- ST 2.1c Time spent on primary activities, total population, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- ST 2.2a1 Time spent on primary activities, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 2.2a2 Time spent on primary activities, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 2.2b1 Time spent on primary activities, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 2.2b2 Time spent on primary activities, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 2.2c1 Time spent on primary activities, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 2.2c2 Time spent on primary activities, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.1a Time spent with others, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.1b Time spent with others, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.2a Time spent with others, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.2b Time spent with others, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.3a Time spent with others, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 4.3b Time spent with others, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10

### Supplementary table 9
Table 9 covers participation rates and mean time spent per day for participants. Included are:
- ST 9.1a1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.1a2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.1b1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.1b2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.1c1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.1c2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2a1 Time spent on primary activities by male participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2a2 Time spent on primary activities by male participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2b1 Time spent on primary activities by female participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2b2 Time spent on primary activities by female participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2c1 Time spent on primary activities by all participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- ST 9.2c2 Time spent on primary activities by all participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10

### Sampling error supplementary tables 2 and 4
Sampling error tables are also available in Excel format. They contain either the relative sampling errors (RSEs) or sampling errors (SEs) for data in the supplementary tables above. They include:
- RSE ST 2.1a Time spent on primary activities, all males, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- RSE ST 2.1b Time spent on primary activities, all females, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- RSE ST 2.1c Time spent on primary activities, total population, by selected demographic characteristics, 1998/99
- RSE ST 2.2a1 Time spent on primary activities, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 2.2a2 Time spent on primary activities, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 2.2b1 Time spent on primary activities, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 2.2b2 Time spent on primary activities, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 2.2c1 Time spent on primary activities, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 2.2c2 Time spent on primary activities, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.1a Time spent with others, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.1b Time spent with others, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.2a Time spent with others, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.2b Time spent with others, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.3a Time spent with others, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 4.3b Time spent with others, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10

### Sampling error supplementary table 9
- SE ST 9.1a1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all males aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- SE ST 9.1a2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all males aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- SE ST 9.1b1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all females aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- SE ST 9.1b2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, all females aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- SE ST 9.1c1 Daily participation rates for primary activities, total population aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- SE ST 9.1c2 Daily participation rates for primary activities, total population aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2a1 Time spent on primary activities by male participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2a2 Time spent on primary activities by male participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2b1 Time spent on primary activities by female participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2b2 Time spent on primary activities by female participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2c1 Time spent on primary activities by all participants aged 12 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- RSE ST 9.2c2 Time spent on primary activities by all participants aged 15 years and over, by selected demographic characteristics, 2009/10
- Statistics for university staff and students 13 January 2021
